title: racosperma crenulatum
intro: Racosperma crenulatum, also known as the Golden Wattle, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is native to Australia and has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Racosperma crenulatum is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for its medicinal uses.
